name: Attila Zoller
abstract: Attila Cornelius Zoller (June 13, 1927  – January 25, 1998) was a Hungarian jazz guitarist. After World War II, he escaped the Soviet takeover of Hungary by fleeing through the mountains on foot into Austria.

concerts.label: Benefit Concert for the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: I played many situations with Albert in the early years with Attila Zoller.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sheila Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: Attila Zoller? ... Wonderful guy. Loved him. ... Eugene said, “No, let her try,” ... So it so happened that I… they didn‟t have a place to put me so I was in a church. about_quotes.doc_title: Sheila Jordan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Genevieve Rose
about_quotes.evidence: I did the Vermont Jazz Summer Workshop, and that ... was Attila Zoller’s Jazz Workshop... I’d say that was a very big influence there. Especially meeting people in person.
about_quotes.doc_title: Genevieve Rose interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: I got to know him there and... I think John Lewis was a big help, too, and John got him up to the music school up in Lenox... Attila was in the group—Attila Zoller.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
